items.passage: Wei Ming kept glancing at the clock and tapping his foot. He had not heard from his sister, who had promised to call an hour ago.
items.stem: How was Wei Ming most likely feeling?
items.rationale: Glancing at the clock and tapping his foot over a late call show he was worried.

items.passage: The classroom fell silent the moment the door opened. All the pupils sat up straight and put their storybooks away.
items.stem: Who most likely walked in?
items.rationale: Pupils falling silent and sitting up straight suggests the teacher walked in.

items.passage: Siti carried an umbrella to school even though the sky was clear. On the news that morning, the weatherman had warned of afternoon showers.
items.stem: Why did Siti bring an umbrella?
items.rationale: The warning of afternoon showers explains why she brought it despite the clear sky.

items.passage: Ravi's hands trembled as he stepped onto the stage. He took a deep breath, looked at the crowd, and began to speak.
items.stem: What can we tell about Ravi?

choices.label: He was nervous but tried to stay calm.
He was bored by the event.
He did not want to be there.

items.rationale: Trembling hands show nerves; taking a deep breath shows he tried to stay calm.

items.passage: Kavitha shared her sandwich with a new pupil who had forgotten his lunch. She also showed him the way to the canteen.
items.stem: What kind of person is Kavitha?
items.rationale: Sharing her food and helping the new pupil show she is thoughtful.

items.passage: The plates were empty and the guests leaned back with satisfied smiles. Mrs Tan began clearing the table as everyone thanked her.
items.stem: What had most likely just happened?

choices.label: The guests had finished a good meal.
The meal had just been served.

items.rationale: Empty plates and satisfied smiles show the guests had finished a good meal.

items.passage: Ahmad wrapped himself in a thick blanket and turned on the heater. He rubbed his hands together to keep them warm.
items.stem: What was the weather like?
items.rationale: A thick blanket, the heater and rubbing his hands show it was cold.

items.passage: Nurul had studied hard all week. When the results were read out, she jumped up with a huge grin and hugged her best friend.
items.stem: What can we conclude?

choices.label: She did very well in the test.
She was not interested in the results.

items.rationale: Jumping up with a grin and hugging her friend show she did very well.

items.passage: The puppy wagged its tail and ran in circles the moment Meiling picked up its leash.
items.stem: How did the puppy feel?

choices.label: Excited to go for a walk

items.rationale: Wagging its tail and running in circles at the leash show it was excited for a walk.

items.passage: Mr Lim checked the price twice, put the toy back on the shelf, and walked out of the shop empty-handed.
items.stem: Why did Mr Lim not buy the toy?

choices.label: He felt it was too expensive.

items.rationale: Checking the price twice before leaving empty-handed suggests he found it too expensive.

items.passage: The field was muddy and the players were covered in dirt, but they cheered loudly as they lifted the trophy.
items.stem: What can we tell about the match?

choices.label: The team won despite the muddy field.

items.rationale: Lifting the trophy and cheering show they won, even on a muddy field.

items.passage: Aisha peeked through the curtains every few minutes. On the table sat a wrapped gift and a cake with candles ready to be lit.
items.stem: What was Aisha waiting for?

choices.label: A guest to arrive for a party

items.rationale: A wrapped gift, a cake with candles, and peeking out the window point to waiting for a party guest.

items.passage: The bus was packed and Ravi stood the whole way, holding the rail tightly as it swayed round each corner.
items.stem: Why did Ravi hold the rail tightly?
items.rationale: Holding the rail as the bus swayed shows he was keeping his balance.

items.passage: Grandpa put on his glasses, held the letter close, and read it slowly. A tear rolled down his cheek and he smiled.
items.stem: How did the letter make Grandpa feel?
items.rationale: A tear together with a smile shows he was touched and happy.

items.passage: By the time the bell rang, Siti's page was still blank. She had spent the lesson staring out of the window.
items.stem: What can we tell about Siti during the lesson?
items.rationale: A blank page and staring out of the window show she was not paying attention.

items.passage: The lights in the flat flickered, then went out. Outside, the whole block was dark and neighbours began calling out to one another.
items.stem: What had happened?

choices.label: There was a power cut in the block.

items.rationale: The whole block being dark shows a power cut, not just one flat.

items.passage: Meiling tiptoed past the bedroom and shut the door softly. Inside, her baby brother was fast asleep.
items.stem: Why did Meiling move so quietly?

choices.label: So she would not wake the baby

items.rationale: Tiptoeing and shutting the door softly near a sleeping baby show she did not want to wake him.

items.passage: Ahmad handed his pocket money to the stallholder and pointed at the wilting plant in the corner. 'I'll take that one,' he said with a gentle smile.
items.stem: What does this show about Ahmad?
items.rationale: Choosing to buy the dying plant to care for it shows he is kind and caring.

items.passage: The queue outside the clinic grew longer and longer. Many people were coughing and some had wrapped scarves around their necks.
items.stem: What was most likely going around?
items.rationale: Coughing people queuing at a clinic suggest a cold or flu was going around.

items.passage: Nurul returned the extra change the cashier had given her by mistake, even though no one had noticed.
items.stem: What lesson does Nurul's action show?

choices.label: Being honest even when no one is watching

items.rationale: Returning the change when no one noticed shows honesty even when unwatched.

CM-inference.iCanStatement: I can work out answers that the passage does not say directly.
CM-inference.explanation: Some answers are not written in the passage — you must be a detective and use clues. Look at what people do, say and feel, then work out the most sensible answer. Always check that your answer fits every clue in the passage.

workedExamples.text: He rubbed his hands and put on a blanket.
workedExamples.note: The clues tell you it was cold, even though the word 'cold' is never used.

workedExamples.text: She returned the extra change no one noticed.
workedExamples.note: Her action shows she is honest — that is the point of the passage.
